=============================================================================\n// ValidateStatusCodeInt Tests (Basic integer validation)\n// int\n\t\tactual   int\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"200 OK matches 200\", 200, 200},\n\t\t{\"404 Not Found matches 404\", 404, 404},\n\t\t{\"403 Forbidden matches 403\", 403, 403},\n\t\t{\"500 Internal Server Error matches 302\", 302, 302},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := ValidateStatusCodeInt(tt.expected, tt.actual)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ValidateStatusCodeInt(%d, %d) expected nil, got error: %v\",\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.expected, tt.actual, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodeInt_NonMatchingCodes(t TestValidateStatusCodeInt_ErrorMessageFormat(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that error message follows the expected format\n\terr := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 404)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected non-nil error for mismatched codes\")\n\t}\n\n\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t// Check for key components of the error message (new format)\n\texpectedParts := []string{\n\t\t\"status_code validation failed\",\n\t\t\"Expected:\",\n\t\t\"200\", // Expected code\n\t\t\"OK\", // Description for 200\n\t\t\"Received:\",\n\t\t\"404\", // Actual code\n\t\t\"Not Found\", // Description for 404\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, part := range expectedParts {\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, part) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message should contain '%s', got: %s\", part, errMsg)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify it's a ValidationError type\n\tif _, ok := err.(validate.ValidationError); !ok {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error should be a validate.ValidationError type\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// tt.actual)\n\n\t\t\tif tt.shouldPass {\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected nil, got error: %v\", tt.description, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tt.description)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodePattern_4xxRange(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tpattern       string\n\t\tactual        int\n\t\tshouldPass    bool\n\t\tdescription   string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"400 Bad Request matches 4xx\", \"4xx\", 400, true, \"Client Error - Bad Request\"},\n\t\t{\"401 Unauthorized matches 4xx\", \"4xx\", 401, true, \"Client Error - Unauthorized\"},\n\t\t{\"403 Forbidden matches 4xx\", \"4xx\", 403, true, \"Client Error - Forbidden\"},\n\t\t{\"404 Not Found matches 4xx\", \"4xx\", 404, true, \"Client Error - Not Found\"},\n\t\t{\"409 Conflict matches 4xx\", \"4xx\", 409, true, \"Client Error - Conflict\"},\n\t\t{\"422 Requests\"},\n\t\t{\"499 matches 4xx\", \"4xx\", 499, true, \"Client Error - upper bound\"},\n\t\t{\"200 OK does not match 4xx\", \"4xx\", 200, false, \"Success - out of range\"},\n\t\t{\"300 Redirect does not match 4xx\", \"4xx\", 300, false, \"Redirection - out of range\"},\n\t\t{\"500 Server Error does not match 4xx\", \"4xx\", 500, false, \"Server Error - out of range\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := expected nil, got error: %v\", tt.description, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tt.description)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodePattern_5xxRange(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tpattern       string\n\t\tactual        int\n\t\tshouldPass    bool\n\t\tdescription   string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"500 Internal Server Error matches 5xx\", \"5xx\", 500, true, \"Server Error - Internal Server tt.description)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodePattern_InvalidPatterns(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tpattern       string\n\t\tactual        int\n\t\terrorContains string\n\t\tdescription   string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"Empty pattern\", \"\", 200, \"invalid status code pattern\", \"Empty string is invalid\"},\n\t\t{\"Pattern too short\", \"4x\", 404, \"must be in format 'Nxx'\", \"Too short\"},\n\t\t{\"Pattern too long\", \"4xxx\", 404, \"must be in format 'Nxx'\", \"Too long\"},\n\t\t{\"Pattern without xx\", \"4yy\", 404, \"must be in format 'Nxx'\", \"Wrong suffix\"},\n\t\t{\"Pattern with uppercase XX\", \"4XX\", 404, \"must be in format 'Nxx'\", \"Uppercase not supported\"},\n\t\t{\"Pattern with space\", \"4 xx\", 404, \"must be in format 'Nxx'\", \"Space in pattern\"},\n\t\t{\"Invalid century 0\", \"0xx\", 200, \"century digit must be 1-5\", \"0 is not a valid HTTP century\"},\n\t\t{\"Invalid century 6\", \"6xx\", 600, \"century digit must be 1-5\", \"6 is not a valid HTTP century\"},\n\t\t{\"Invalid century 9\", \"9xx\", 900, \"century digit must be 1-5\", \"9 is not a valid HTTP century\"},\n\t\t{\"Non-numeric prefix\", \"axx\", 200, \"century digit must be 1-5\", \"Letter prefix is invalid\"},\n\t\t{\"Special characters\", \"*xx\", 200, \"century digit must be 1-5\", \"Special characters are invalid\"},\n\t\t{\"Random string\", \"invalid\", 200, \"must be in format 'Nxx'\", \"Random string is invalid\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := ValidateStatusCodePattern(tt.pattern, tt.actual)\n\n\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tt.description)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Verify error message contains helpful information\n\t\t\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\t\t\t\tif tt.errorContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(errMsg, tt.errorContains) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message should contain '%s', got: %s\", tt.errorContains, errMsg)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Error message should include the invalid pattern for debugging\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, tt.pattern) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message should include pattern '%s', got: %s\", tt.pattern, errMsg)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodePattern_ErrorMessageFormat(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tpattern       string\n\t\tactual        int\n\t\terrorContains []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"4xx pattern with 200 actual\",\n\t\t\tpattern: \"4xx\",\n\t\t\tactual:  200,\n\t\t\terrorContains: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"200\",\n\t\t\t\t\"OK\",\n\t\t\t\t\"4xx\",\n\t\t\t\t\"400-499\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"5xx pattern with 404 actual\",\n\t\t\tpattern: \"5xx\",\n\t\t\tactual:  404,\n\t\t\terrorContains: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"404\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Not Found\",\n\t\t\t\t\"5xx\",\n\t\t\t\t\"500-599\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"2xx pattern with 500 actual\",\n\t\t\tpattern: \"2xx\",\n\t\t\tactual:  500,\n\t\t\terrorContains: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"500\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Internal Server Error\",\n\t\t\t\t\"2xx\",\n\t\t\t\t\"200-299\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := ValidateStatusCodePattern(tt.pattern, tt.actual)\n\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected non-nil error for non-matching pattern\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\t\t\tfor _, expected := range tt.errorContains {\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, expected) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message should contain '%s', got: %s\", expected, errMsg)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodePattern_EdgeCases(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\tpattern     string\n\t\tactual      int\n\t\tshouldPass  bool\n\t\tdescription string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"Lower boundary of 1xx\", \"1xx\", 100, true, \"100 is in 1xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Upper boundary of 1xx\", \"1xx\", 199, true, \"199 is in 1xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just below 1xx\", \"1xx\", 99, false, \"99 is below 1xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just above 1xx\", \"1xx\", 200, false, \"200 is above 1xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Lower boundary of 2xx\", \"2xx\", 200, true, \"200 is in 2xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Upper boundary of 2xx\", \"2xx\", 299, true, \"299 is in 2xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just below 2xx\", \"2xx\", 199, false, \"199 is below 2xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just above 2xx\", \"2xx\", 300, false, \"300 is above 2xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Lower boundary of 3xx\", \"3xx\", 300, true, \"300 is in 3xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Upper boundary of 3xx\", \"3xx\", 399, true, \"399 is in 3xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just below 3xx\", \"3xx\", 299, false, \"299 is below 3xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just above 3xx\", \"3xx\", 400, false, \"400 is above 3xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Lower boundary of 4xx\", \"4xx\", 400, true, \"400 is in 4xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Upper boundary of 4xx\", \"4xx\", 499, true, \"499 is in 4xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just below 4xx\", \"4xx\", 399, false, \"399 is below 4xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just above 4xx\", \"4xx\", 500, false, \"500 is above 4xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Lower boundary of 5xx\", \"5xx\", 500, true, \"500 is in 5xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Upper boundary of 5xx\", \"5xx\", 599, true, \"599 is in 5xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just below 5xx\", \"5xx\", 499, false, \"499 is below 5xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Just above 5xx\", \"5xx\", 600, false, \"600 is above 5xx range\"},\n\t\t{\"Zero status code\", \"2xx\", 0, false, \"0 is not a valid HTTP status code\"},\n\t\t{\"Negative status code\", \"2xx\", -1, false, \"Negative codes are invalid\"},\n\t\t{\"Very large status code\", \"5xx\", 9999, false, \"9999 is out of HTTP range\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := ValidateStatusCodePattern(tt.pattern, tt.actual)\n\n\t\t\tif tt.shouldPass {\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected nil, got error: %v\", tt.description, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tt.description)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestParseStatusCodePattern_ValidPatterns(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tpattern  string\n\t\texpected int\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"1xx\", 1},\n\t\t{\"2xx\", 2},\n\t\t{\"3xx\", 3},\n\t\t{\"4xx\", 4},\n\t\t{\"5xx\", 5},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.pattern, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tcentury, err := parseStatusCodePattern(tt.pattern)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"parseStatusCodePattern(%s) unexpected error: %v\", tt.pattern, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif century != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"parseStatusCodePattern(%s) = %d, want %d\", tt.pattern, century, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestParseStatusCodePattern_InvalidPatterns(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tpattern        string\n\t\terrorContains  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"\", \"must be in format\"},\n\t\t{\"4x\", \"must be in format\"},\n\t\t{\"4xxx\", \"must be in format\"},\n\t\t{\"4yy\", \"must be in format\"},\n\t\t{\"4XX\", \"must be in format\"},\n\t\t{\"0xx\", \"century digit must be 1-5\"},\n\t\t{\"6xx\", \"century digit must be 1-5\"},\n\t\t{\"9xx\", \"century digit must be 1-5\"},\n\t\t{\"axx\", \"century digit must be 1-5\"},\n\t\t{\"invalid\", \"must be in format\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.pattern, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tcentury, err := parseStatusCodePattern(tt.pattern)\n\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"parseStatusCodePattern(%s) expected error, got nil (century=%d)\", tt.pattern, century)\n\t\t\t} else if tt.errorContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorContains) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error should contain '%s', got: %s\", tt.errorContains, err.Error())\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CheckStatusCode Tests (Non-asserting versions)\n// TestCheckStatusCodeRange_Range(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tresponseCode  int\n\t\tminCode       int\n\t\tmaxCode       int\n\t\texpectedResult tt.maxCode, result, tt.expectedResult)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// GetStatusCodeDescription Tests\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestGetStatusCodeDescription_KnownCodes(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tcode        int\n\t\tdescription string\n\t}{\n\t\t{100, \"Continue\"},\n\t\t{200, \"OK\"},\n\t\t{201, \"Created\"},\n\t\t{204, \"No Content\"},\n\t\t{301, \"Moved Permanently\"},\n\t\t{302, It now uses the validation error\n38\t// formatting helper to provide structured error messages with auto-generated\n39\t// suggestions and consistent formatting.\n40\t//\n41\t// Parameters:\n42\t//   - expected: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n43\t//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n44\t//\n45\t// Returns:\n46\t//   - nil if the status codes match\n47\t//   - error with descriptive message if they don't match\n48\t//\n49\t// Example (matching codes):\n50\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 200)\n51\t//   // err == nil\n52\t//\n53\t// Example (non-matching codes):\n54\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 404)\n55\t//   // err.Error() contains structured error with suggestions\n56\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeInt(expected, actual int) error {\n57\t\tif expected == actual {\n58\t\t\treturn nil\n59\t\t}\n60\t\t// Use the validation error formatting helper for enhanced error messages\n61\t\tvalidationError := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n62\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n63\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n64\t\t\tWithContext(\"HTTP status code validation\").\n65\t\t\tFormat()\n66\t\n67\t\treturn validationError\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t// ValidateStatusCodeIntWithContext validates expected vs actual HTTP status codes with context.\n71\t//\n72\t// This is an enhanced version of ValidateStatusCodeInt that accepts HTTP method and endpoint\n73\t// information to provide more detailed error messages. GetStatusCodeDescription(expected)))\n382\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n383\t\n384\t\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n385\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n386\t\t}\n387\t\n388\t\treturn b.String()\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchError creates an enhanced error message using validation error formatting.\n392\t//\n393\t// This helper uses the validate package's ValidationError to create structured, detailed error messages\n394\t// with auto-generated suggestions and consistent formatting.\n395\tfunc buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchError(expected, actual int, httpContext string) validate.ValidationError {\n396\t\tcontextStr := httpContext\n397\t\tif contextStr == \"\" {\n398\t\t\tcontextStr = \"HTTP request\"\n399\t\t}\n400\t\n401\t\treturn validate.FormatStatusCodeError(expected, actual, contextStr)\n402\t}\n403\t\n404\t// buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates an enhanced error message for multiple allowed codes.\n405\t//\n406\t// This helper creates structured error messages when validating against multiple allowed status codes.\n407\tfunc buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedCodes []int, actual int, httpContext string) validate.ValidationError {\n408\t\tcontextStr := httpContext\n409\t\tif contextStr == \"\" {\n410\t\t\tcontextStr = \"HTTP request\"\n411\t\t}\n412\t\n413\t\treturn validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n414\t\t\tWithExpected(allowedCodes).\n415\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n416\t\t\tWithContext(contextStr).\n417\t\t\tFormat()\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t// buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates a detailed error message for multi-code mismatches.\n421\t//\n422\t// This helper builds a comprehensive error message when validating against multiple\n423\t// allowed status codes, showing all allowed codes vs the actual received code.\n424\tfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedList string, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n425\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n426\t\n427\t\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n428\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: one of [%s]\\n\", allowedList))\n429\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n430\t\n431\t\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n432\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n433\t\t}\n434\t\n435\t\treturn b.String()\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\t// getStatusCodeResponseContext extracts contextual information about the response object.\n439\t//\n440\t// This helper builds a string description of the response object for error messages.\n441\t// For status code validation, the context includes the response type.\n442\tfunc getStatusCodeResponseContext(response interface{}) string {\n443\t\tswitch response.(type) {\n444\t\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"httptest.ResponseRecorder\")\n446\t\tcase *http.Response:\n447\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"http.Response\")\n448\t\tdefault:\n449\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown type: %T\", response)\n450\t\t}\n451\t}\n452\t\n453\t// =============================================================================\n454\t// HTTP CONTEXT EXTRACTION\n455\t// =============================================================================\n456\t\n457\t// HTTPContext contains information about an HTTP request for error messages.\n458\ttype HTTPContext struct {\n459\t\t// Method is the HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n460\t\tMethod string\n461\t\n462\t\t// Endpoint is the request path/URL\n463\t\tEndpoint string\n464\t\n465\t\t// Description is an optional additional description\n466\t\tDescription string\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t// String formats the HTTP context as \"METHOD /endpoint\" or with description.\n470\tfunc (h HTTPContext) String() string {\n471\t\tvar parts []string\n472\t\tif h.Method != \"\" {\n473\t\t\tparts = append(parts, h.Method)\n474\t\t}\n475\t\tif h.Endpoint != \"\" {\n476\t\t\tparts = append(parts, h.Endpoint)\n477\t\t}\n478\t\n479\t\tbase := strings.Join(parts, \" \")\n480\t\tif h.Description != \"\" {\n481\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", base, h.Description)\n482\t\t}\n483\t\treturn base\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// ExtractHTTPContext extracts HTTP method and endpoint from a request.\n487\t//\n488\t// This helper function extracts context information from http.Request for use\n489\t// in validation error messages. If the request is nil, it returns an empty context.\n490\t//\n491\t// Parameters:\n492\t//   - req: The HTTP request to extract context from\n493\t//\n494\t// Returns an HTTPContext struct with method, endpoint, and optional description.\n495\tfunc ExtractHTTPContext(req *http.Request) HTTPContext {\n496\t\tif req == nil {\n497\t\t\treturn HTTPContext{}\n498\t\t}\n499\t\n500\t\treturn HTTPContext{\n501\t\t\tMethod:   req.Method,\n502\t\t\tEndpoint: req.URL.Path,\n503\t\t}\n504\t}\n505\t\n506\t// ExtractHTTPContextFromRecorder extracts HTTP context from a test recorder.\n507\t//\n508\t// This helper extracts HTTP context information from a test response recorder\n509\t// by accessing the underlying request if available.\n510\t//\n511\t// Parameters:\n512\t//   - recorder: The httptest.ResponseRecorder to extract context from\n513\t//\n514\t// Returns an HTTPContext struct with method and endpoint.\n515\tfunc ExtractHTTPContextFromRecorder(recorder *httptest.ResponseRecorder) HTTPContext {\n516\t\tif recorder == nil {\n517\t\t\treturn HTTPContext{}\n518\t\t}\n519\t\n520\t\t// ResponseRecorder doesn't store the request, so we can't extract it\n521\t\t// Users should pass the request explicitly if they want HTTP context\n522\t\treturn HTTPContext{}\n523\t}\n524\t\n525\t// FormatHTTPContext formats an HTTPContext into a string for error messages.\n526\t//\n527\t// This is a convenience function that formats HTTP context information\n528\t// into a readable string for use in validation error messages.\n529\t//\n530\t// Parameters:\n531\t//   - ctx: The HTTPContext to format\n532\t//\n533\t// Returns a formatted string like \"GET /api/users\" or empty string if context is empty.\n534\tfunc FormatHTTPContext(ctx HTTPContext) string {\n535\t\treturn ctx.String()\n536\t}\n537\t\n538\t// formatCodeList formats a list of status codes with descriptions for error messages.\n539\t//\n540\t// This helper creates a readable string representation of multiple allowed codes,\n541\t// including their descriptions for better debugging.\n542\tfunc formatCodeList(codes []int) string {\n543\t\tvar parts []string\n544\t\tfor _, code := range codes {\n545\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n546\t\t}\n547\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n548\t}\n549\t\n550\t// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n551\t//\n552\t// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n553\t// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n554\t// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n555\t//\n556\t// Parameters:\n557\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n558\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n559\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n560\t//\n561\t// Example:\n562\t//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n563\tfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n564\t\tt.Helper()\n565\t\n566\t\tresult := AssertStatusCode(response, expectedCode, true)\n567\t\n568\t\tif !result.Match {\n569\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: %d (%s)\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n570\t\t\t\tresult.Expected, result.ExpectedDescription,\n571\t\t\t\tresult.Actual, result.ActualDescription,\n572\t\t\t\tresult.ResponseContext)\n573\t\t}\n574\t}\n575\t\n576\t// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n577\t//\n578\t// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n579\t// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n580\t// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n581\t//\n582\t// Parameters:\n583\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n584\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n585\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n586\t//\n587\t// Example:\n588\t//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\n589\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n590\t\tt.Helper()\n591\t\n592\t\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n593\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n594\t\t\treturn\n595\t\t}\n596\t\n597\t\tresult := AssertStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes, true)\n598\t\n599\t\tif !result.Match {\n600\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: one of [%s]\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n601\t\t\t\tresult.ExpectedDescription, result.Actual, result.ActualDescription, result.ResponseContext)\n602\t\t}\n603\t}\n604\t\n605\t// ValidateStatusCodePattern validates that an HTTP status code matches a pattern.\n606\t//\n607\t// This function validates status codes using pattern strings like '4xx', '5xx', etc.\n608\t// It extracts the century digit from the pattern and validates that the actual code\n609\t// falls within the corresponding range (e.g., '4xx' validates codes 400-499).\n610\t//\n611\t// Parameters:\n612\t//   - pattern: The status code pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx')\n613\t//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n614\t//\n615\t// Returns:\n616\t//   - nil if the status code matches the pattern\n617\t//   - error with descriptive message if pattern is invalid or code doesn't match\n618\t//\n619\t// Example (matching pattern):\n620\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 404)\n621\t//   // err == nil\n622\t//\n623\t// Example (non-matching pattern):\n624\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 200)\n625\t//   // err.Error() == \"status code 200 (OK) does not match pattern '4xx' (expected 400-499)\"\n626\t//\n627\t// Example (invalid pattern):\n628\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('invalid', 200)\n629\t//   // err.Error() == \"invalid status code pattern 'invalid': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\"\n630\tfunc ValidateStatusCodePattern(pattern string, actual int) error {\n631\t\t// Parse the pattern to extract the century digit\n632\t\tcentury, err := parseStatusCodePattern(pattern)\n633\t\tif err != nil {\n634\t\t\treturn err\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\t// Calculate the expected range for this century\n638\t\tminCode := century * 100\n639\t\tmaxCode := minCode + 99\n640\t\n641\t\t// Validate the actual code falls within the range\n642\t\tif actual >= minCode && actual <= maxCode {\n643\t\t\treturn nil\n644\t\t}\n645\t\n646\t\t// Build descriptive error message\n647\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"status code %d (%s) does not match pattern '%s' (expected %d-%d)\",\n648\t\t\tactual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual), pattern, minCode, maxCode)\n649\t}\n650\t\n651\t// parseStatusCodePattern parses a status code pattern like '4xx' and returns the century digit.\n652\t//\n653\t// This helper extracts the century digit from patterns in the format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5.\n654\t// It validates the pattern format and returns an error for invalid patterns.\n655\t//\n656\t// Parameters:\n657\t//   - pattern: The status code pattern to parse (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n658\t//\n659\t// Returns:\n660\t//   - The century digit (1-5) if the pattern is valid\n661\t//   - error if the pattern is invalid\n662\t//\n663\t// Example:\n664\t//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('4xx')\n665\t//   // century == 4, err == nil\n666\t//\n667\t// Example (invalid pattern):\n668\t//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('invalid')\n669\t//   // century == 0, err != nil\n670\tfunc parseStatusCodePattern(pattern string) (int, error) {\n671\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n672\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n673\t\t}\n674\t\n675\t\t// Check if pattern ends with 'xx'\n676\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n677\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n678\t\t}\n679\t\n680\t\t// Extract the century digit\n681\t\tcentury := int(pattern[0] - '0')\n682\t\n683\t\t// Validate century is in valid HTTP range (1-5)\n684\t\tif century < 1 || century > 5 {\n685\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': century digit must be 1-5\", pattern)\n686\t\t}\n687\t\n688\t\treturn century, nil\n689\t}\n690\t\n691\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n692\t//\n693\t// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n694\t// - 2xx for success responses (200-299)\n695\t// - 4xx for client errors (400-499)\n696\t// - 5xx for server errors (500-599)\n697\t//\n698\t// Parameters:\n699\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n700\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n701\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n702\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n703\t//\n704\t// Example:\n705\t//   // Validate 2xx success response\n706\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n707\t//\n708\t//   // Validate 4xx client error\n709\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499)\n710\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(t *testing.T, response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) {\n711\t\tt.Helper()\n712\t\n713\t\tif minCode > maxCode {\n714\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ValidateStatusCodeRange: minCode (%d) cannot be greater than maxCode (%d)\", minCode, maxCode)\n715\t\t}\n716\t\n717\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n718\t\n719\t\tif actualCode < minCode || actualCode > maxCode {\n720\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code in range [%d-%d] (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n721\t\t\t\tminCode, maxCode, getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode),\n722\t\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n723\t\t}\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n727\t//\n728\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n729\t// in tests. Useful when you need to handle different status codes programmatically\n730\t// rather than failing the test immediately.\n731\t//\n732\t// Parameters:\n733\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n734\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n735\t//\n736\t// Returns:\n737\t//   - true if the response has the expected status code, false otherwise\n738\t//\n739\t// Example:\n740\t//   if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n741\t//       // Handle 404 case\n742\t//   } else {\n743\t//       // Handle other cases\n744\t//   }\n745\tfunc CheckStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n746\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n747\t\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n748\t}\n749\t\n750\t// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n751\t//\n752\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n753\t// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n754\t//\n755\t// Parameters:\n756\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n757\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n758\t//\n759\t// Returns:\n760\t//   - true if the response status code is in the allowed list, false otherwise\n761\t//\n762\t// Example:\n763\t//   allowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n764\t//   if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n765\t//       // Handle success case\n766\t//   } else {\n767\t//       // Handle error case\n768\t//   }\n769\tfunc CheckStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) bool {\n770\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n771\t\n772\t\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n773\t\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n774\t\t\t\treturn true\n775\t\t\t}\n776\t\t}\n777\t\treturn false\n778\t}\n779\t\n780\t// CheckStatusCodeRange checks if the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n781\t//\n782\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for range-based status code validation.\n783\t// Useful for category-based status code checking (e.g., 2xx, 4xx, 5xx).\n784\t//\n785\t// Parameters:\n786\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n787\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n788\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n789\t//\n790\t// Returns:\n791\t//   - true if the status code is within the range, false otherwise\n792\t//\n793\t// Example:\n794\t//   // Check for any success code (2xx)\n795\t//   if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n796\t//       // Handle success\n797\t//   }\n798\tfunc CheckStatusCodeRange(response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) bool {\n799\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n800\t\treturn actualCode >= minCode && actualCode <= maxCode\n801\t}\n802\t\n803\t// GetStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n804\t//\n805\t// This helper provides descriptive names for common HTTP status codes, making\n806\t// test error messages more informative and easier to understand.\n807\t//\n808\t// Parameters:\n809\t//   - code: The HTTP status code\n810\t//\n811\t// Returns:\n812\t//   - A human-readable description string (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n813\t//\n814\t// Example:\n815\t//   desc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n816\t//   // desc = \"Not Found\"\n817\tfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n818\t\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n819\t\t\t100: \"Continue\",\n820\t\t\t101: \"Switching Protocols\",\n821\t\t\t102: \"Processing\",\n822\t\t\t200: \"OK\",\n823\t\t\t201: \"Created\",\n824\t\t\t202: \"Accepted\",\n825\t\t\t203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n826\t\t\t204: \"No Content\",\n827\t\t\t205: \"Reset Content\",\n828\t\t\t206: \"Partial Content\",\n829\t\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\",\n830\t\t\t301: \"Moved Permanently\",\n831\t\t\t302: \"Found\",\n832\t\t\t303: \"See Other\",\n833\t\t\t304: \"Not Modified\",\n834\t\t\t305: \"Use Proxy\",\n835\t\t\t307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n836\t\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n837\t\t\t400: \"Bad Request\",\n838\t\t\t401: \"Unauthorized\",\n839\t\t\t402: \"Payment Required\",\n840\t\t\t403: \"Forbidden\",\n841\t\t\t404: \"Not Found\",\n842\t\t\t405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n843\t\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\",\n844\t\t\t407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n845\t\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\",\n846\t\t\t409: \"Conflict\",\n847\t\t\t410: \"Gone\",\n848\t\t\t411: \"Length Required\",\n849\t\t\t412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n850\t\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\",\n851\t\t\t414: \"URI Too Long\",\n852\t\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n853\t\t\t416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n854\t\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\",\n855\t\t\t418: \"I'm a teapot\",\n856\t\t\t422: \"Unprocessable Entity\",\n857\t\t\t423: \"Locked\",\n858\t\t\t424: \"Failed Dependency\",\n859\t\t\t426: \"Upgrade Required\",\n860\t\t\t428: \"Precondition Required\",\n861\t\t\t429: \"Too Many Requests\",\n862\t\t\t431: \"Request Header Fields Too Large\",\n863\t\t\t451: \"Unavailable For Legal Reasons\",\n864\t\t\t500: \"Internal Server Error\",\n865\t\t\t501: \"Not Implemented\",\n866\t\t\t502: \"Bad Gateway\",\n867\t\t\t503: \"Service Unavailable\",\n868\t\t\t504: \"Gateway Timeout\",\n869\t\t\t505: \"HTTP Version Not Supported\",\n870\t\t\t506: \"Variant Also Negotiates\",\n871\t\t\t507: \"Insufficient Storage\",\n872\t\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\",\n873\t\t\t510: \"Not Extended\",\n874\t\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n875\t\t}\n876\t\n877\t\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n878\t\t\treturn desc\n879\t\t}\n880\t\treturn \"Unknown\"\n881\t}\n882\t\n883\t// getRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n884\t//\n885\t// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges,\n886\t// such as \"Success\" for 2xx codes or \"Client Error\" for 4xx codes.\n887\tfunc getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n888\t\tranges := map[string]string{\n889\t\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n890\t\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n891\t\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n892\t\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n893\t\t}\n894\t\n895\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n896\t\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n897\t\t\treturn desc\n898\t\t}\n899\t\n900\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Codes %d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n901\t}\n902\t\n903\t// getStatusCode extracts the HTTP status code from various response types.\n904\t//\n905\t// This helper supports both *httptest.ResponseRecorder and *http.Response,\n906\t// making the validation functions work with different response types.\n907\tfunc getStatusCode(response interface{}) int {\n908\t\tswitch r := response.(type) {\n909\t\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n910\t\t\treturn r.Code\n911\t\tcase *http.Response:\n912\t\t\treturn r.StatusCode\n913\t\tdefault:\n914\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"Unsupported response type: %T. Expected *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response\", response))\n915\t\t}\n916\t}\n917\t\n918\t// =============================================================================\n919\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON STATUS CODE CATEGORIES\n920\t// =============================================================================\n921\t\n922\t// ValidateSuccessStatusCode validates that the response has a 2xx success status code.\n923\t//\n924\t// This convenience function validates any successful response (200-299).\n925\t// Common success codes include:\n926\t// - 200: OK\n927\t// - 201: Created\n928\t// - 204: No Content\n929\t//\n930\t// Example:\n931\t//   ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t, w)\n932\tfunc ValidateSuccessStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}) {\n933\t\tt.Helper()\n934\t\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, response, 200, 299)\n935\t}\n936\t\n937\t// ValidateClientErrorStatusCode validates that the response has a 4xx client error status code.\n938\t//\n939\t// This convenience function validates any client error response (400-499).\n940\t// Common client error codes include:\n941\t// - =============================================================================\n// HTTP STATUS CODE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// This module provides comprehensive HTTP status code validation utilities\n// for error response testing. It supports single status codes, multiple allowed\n// status codes, pattern-based range validation, and flexible validation patterns.\n//\n// Functions:\n// - ValidateStatusCodeInt: Basic status code validation with expected/actual ints\n// - ValidateStatusCodePattern: Validate status code matches pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n// - ValidateStatusCode: Validate single expected status code (test helper)\n// - ValidateStatusCodeAny: Validate against multiple allowed status codes\n// - ValidateStatusCodeRange: Validate status code is within range\n// - CheckStatusCode: Non-asserting version that returns boolean\n// - AssertStatusCode: Flexible validation supporting both assertion and boolean modes\n// - AssertStatusCodeAny: Flexible multi-code validation with assertion mode\n// - StatusCodeMatchResult: Detailed validation result with error context\n// - GetStatusCodeDescription: Get human-readable status code description\n// - parseStatusCodePattern: Parse pattern string to extract century digit\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeInt validates expected vs actual HTTP status codes.\n//\n// This is a basic status code validation function that compares two integers\n// and returns an error if they don't match. It now uses the validation error\n// formatting helper to provide structured error messages with auto-generated\n// suggestions and consistent formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the status codes match\n//   - error with descriptive message if they don't match\n//\n// Example (matching codes):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 200)\n//   // err == nil\n//\n// Example (non-matching codes):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 404)\n//   // err.Error() contains structured error with suggestions\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeInt(expected, actual int) error {\n\tif expected == actual {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// Use the validation error formatting helper for enhanced error messages\n\tvalidationError := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(\"HTTP status code validation\").\n\t\tFormat()\n\n\treturn validationError\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeIntWithContext validates expected vs actual HTTP status codes with context.\n//\n// This is an enhanced version of ValidateStatusCodeInt that accepts HTTP method and endpoint\n// information to provide more detailed error messages. It uses the validation error formatting\n// helpers to create structured error messages with suggestions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n//   - httpContext: HTTP context (method, endpoint) for error messages\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the status codes match\n//   - error with descriptive message if they don't match\n//\n// Example (matching codes):\n//   ctx := HTTPContext{Method: \"GET\", Endpoint: \"/api/users\"}\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodeIntWithContext(200, 200,  // err.Error() contains structured error with suggestions and context\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeIntWithContext(expected, actual int, httpContext HTTPContext) error {\n\tif expected == actual {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tcontextStr := FormatHTTPContext(httpContext)\n\tif contextStr == \"\" {\n\t\tcontextStr = \"HTTP status code validation\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the validation error formatting helper for enhanced error messages\n\tvalidationError := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(contextStr).\n\t\tFormat()\n\n\treturn validationError\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeIntAny validates actual status code against multiple allowed codes.\n//\n// This is an enhanced version that validates against multiple acceptable status codes\n// and returns structured error messages with auto-generated suggestions when validation fails.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable status codes\n//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the actual code matches any in the allowed list\n//   - error with descriptive message if they don't match\n//\n// Example (matching codes):\n//   err := contains structured error with suggestions\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeIntAny(allowedCodes []int, actual int) error {\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actual == allowedCode {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the validation error formatting helper for enhanced error messages\n\tvalidationError := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(allowedCodes).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(\"HTTP status code validation\").\n\t\tFormat()\n\n\treturn validationError\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeIntAnyWithContext validates actual status code against multiple allowed codes with context.\n//\n// This is an enhanced version that validates against StatusCodeMatchResult contains detailed information about a status code validation.\n//\n// This type provides comprehensive error context for debugging validation failures,\n// including the expected and actual status codes with their descriptions, the\n// response object context, and a formatted error message.\ntype StatusCodeMatchResult struct {\n\t// Match indicates whether the status code matched successfully\n\tMatch bool\n\n\t// Expected is the status code that was expected\n\tExpected int\n\n\t// Actual is the status code that was received\n\tActual int\n\n\t// ExpectedDescription is the human-readable description of the expected status code\n\tExpectedDescription string\n\n\t// ActualDescription is the human-readable description of the actual status code\n\tActualDescription string\n\n\t// ResponseContext contains information about the response object\n\tResponseContext string\n\n\t// Error is a formatted error message describing the mismatch\n\tError string\n\n\t// ValidationError is the structured validation error (only populated when using enhanced functions)\n\tValidationError validate.ValidationError\n}\n\n// String returns a human-readable representation of the validation result.\nfunc (r StatusCodeMatchResult) String() string {\n\tif r.Match {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code match: %d (%s)\", r.Actual, r.ActualDescription)\n\t}\n\treturn r.Error\n}\n\n// AssertStatusCode validates status code with flexible assertion mode.\n//\n// This function supports both boolean-only mode (for conditional logic) and\n// assertion mode (with descriptive error messages). When assertMode is true\n// and validation fails, it returns a detailed error message showing expected\n// vs actual values along with response context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure; if false, Full error with expected vs actual\n//   }\nfunc AssertStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\t// Build response context for error messages\n\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n\n\t// Check if status codes match\n\tmatches := actualCode == expectedCode\n\n\tif matches {\n\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the validation error formatting helper for enhanced error messages\n\tvalidationError := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expectedCode).\n\t\tWithActual(actualCode).\n\t\tWithContext(responseContext).\n\t\tFormat()\n\n\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\tError:              validationError.Error(),\n\t\tValidationError:    validationError,\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertStatusCodeAny validates status code against multiple allowed codes.\n//\n// This function is similar to 0,\n\t\t\tActual:             0,\n\t\t\tExpectedDescription: \"\",\n\t\t\tActualDescription:   \"\",\n\t\t\tResponseContext:     getStatusCodeResponseContext(response),\n\t\t\tError:              \"AssertStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n\n\t// Check if actual code matches any in the allowed list\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n\t\t\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n\t\t\t\tExpected:           actualCode,\n\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the validation error formatting helper for enhanced error messages\n\tvalidationError := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(allowedCodes).\n\t\tWithActual(actualCode).\n\t\tWithContext(responseContext).\n\t\tFormat()\n\n\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\tExpected:           0,\n\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\tExpectedDescription: formatCodeList(allowedCodes),\n\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\tError:              validationError.Error(),\n\t\tValidationError:    validationError,\n\t}\n}\n\n// buildStatusCodeMismatchError creates a detailed error message for status code mismatches.\n//\n// This helper builds a comprehensive error message that includes:\n// - The expected status code with description\n// - The actual status code with description\n// - Response object context (type and status if available)\nfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchError(expected, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", expected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected)))\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n\n\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n\t}\n\n\treturn b.String()\n}\n\n// buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchError creates an enhanced error message using validation error formatting.\n//\n// This helper uses the validate package's ValidationError to create structured, detailed error messages\n// with auto-generated suggestions and consistent formatting.\nfunc buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchError(expected, actual int, httpContext string) validate.ValidationError {\n\tcontextStr := httpContext\n\tif contextStr == \"\" {\n\t\tcontextStr = \"HTTP request\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn validate.FormatStatusCodeError(expected, actual, contextStr)\n}\n\n// buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates an enhanced error message for multiple allowed codes.\n//\n// This helper creates structured error messages when validating against multiple allowed status codes.\nfunc buildEnhancedStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedCodes []int, actual int, httpContext string) validate.ValidationError {\n\tcontextStr := httpContext\n\tif contextStr == \"\" {\n\t\tcontextStr = \"HTTP request\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn a detailed error message for multi-code mismatches.\n//\n// This helper builds a comprehensive error message when validating against multiple\n// allowed status codes, showing all allowed codes vs the actual received code.\nfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedList string, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: one of [%s]\\n\", allowedList))\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n\n\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n\t}\n\n\treturn b.String()\n}\n\n// getStatusCodeResponseContext extracts contextual information about the response object.\n//\n// This helper builds a string description of the response object for error messages.\n// For status code validation, the context includes the response type.\nfunc getStatusCodeResponseContext(response interface{}) string {\n\tswitch response.(type) {\n\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"httptest.ResponseRecorder\")\n\tcase *http.Response:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"http.Response\")\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown type: %T\", response)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HTTP CONTEXT EXTRACTION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// HTTPContext contains information about an HTTP request for error messages.\ntype HTTPContext struct {\n\t// Method is the HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n\tMethod string\n\n\t// Endpoint is the request path/URL\n\tEndpoint string\n\n\t// Description is an optional additional description\n\tDescription string\n}\n\n// String formats the HTTP context as \"METHOD /endpoint\" or with description.\nfunc (h HTTPContext) String() string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\tif h.Method != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, h.Method)\n\t}\n\tif h.Endpoint != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, h.Endpoint)\n\t}\n\n\tbase := strings.Join(parts, \" \")\n\tif h.Description != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", base, h.Description)\n\t}\n\treturn base\n}\n\n// ExtractHTTPContext extracts HTTP method and endpoint from a request.\n//\n// This helper function extracts context information from http.Request for use\n// in validation error messages. If the request is nil, it returns an empty context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - req: The HTTP request to extract context from\n//\n// Returns an HTTPContext struct with method, endpoint, and optional description.\nfunc ExtractHTTPContext(req *http.Request) HTTPContext {\n\tif req == nil {\n\t\treturn HTTPContext{}\n\t}\n\n\treturn HTTPContext{\n\t\tMethod:   req.Method,\n\t\tEndpoint: req.URL.Path,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ExtractHTTPContextFromRecorder extracts HTTP context from a test recorder.\n//\n// This helper extracts HTTP context information from a test response recorder\n// by accessing the underlying request if available.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - recorder: The httptest.ResponseRecorder to extract context from\n//\n// Returns an HTTPContext struct with method and endpoint.\nfunc ExtractHTTPContextFromRecorder(recorder *httptest.ResponseRecorder) HTTPContext {\n\tif recorder == nil {\n\t\treturn HTTPContext{}\n\t}\n\n\t// ResponseRecorder doesn't store the request, so we can't extract it\n\t// Users should pass the request explicitly if they want HTTP context\n\treturn HTTPContext{}\n}\n\n// FormatHTTPContext formats an HTTPContext into a string for error messages.\n//\n// This is a convenience function that formats HTTP context information\n// into a readable string for use in validation error messages.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ctx: The HTTPContext to format\n//\n// Returns a formatted string like \"GET /api/users\" or empty string if context is empty.\nfunc FormatHTTPContext(ctx HTTPContext) string {\n\treturn ctx.String()\n}\n\n// formatCodeList formats a list of status codes with descriptions for error messages.\n//\n// This helper creates a readable string representation of multiple allowed codes,\n// including their descriptions for better debugging.\nfunc formatCodeList(codes []int) string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor _, code := range codes {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n//\n// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\nfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tresult := AssertStatusCode(response, expectedCode, true)\n\n\tif !result.Match {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: %d (%s)\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n\t\t\tresult.Expected, result.ExpectedDescription,\n\t\t\tresult.Actual, result.ActualDescription,\n\t\t\tresult.ResponseContext)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n//\n// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tresult := AssertStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes, true)\n\n\tif !result.Match {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: one of [%s]\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n\t\t\tresult.ExpectedDescription, result.Actual, result.ActualDescription, result.ResponseContext)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodePattern validates that an HTTP status code matches a pattern.\n//\n// This function validates status codes using pattern strings like '4xx', '5xx', etc.\n// It extracts the century digit from the pattern and validates that the actual code\n// falls within the corresponding range (e.g., '4xx' validates codes 400-499).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The status code pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx')\n//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the status code matches the pattern\n//   - error with descriptive message if pattern is invalid or code doesn't match\n//\n// Example (matching pattern):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 404)\n//   // err == nil\n//\n// Example (non-matching pattern):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 200)\n//   // err.Error() == \"status code 200 (OK) does not match pattern '4xx' (expected 400-499)\"\n//\n// Example (invalid pattern):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('invalid', 200)\n//   // err.Error() == \"invalid status code pattern 'invalid': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\"\nfunc ValidateStatusCodePattern(pattern string, actual int) error {\n\t// Parse the pattern to extract the century digit\n\tcentury, err := parseStatusCodePattern(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate the expected range for this century\n\tminCode := century * 100\n\tmaxCode := minCode + 99\n\n\t// Validate the actual code falls within the range\n\tif actual >= minCode && actual <= maxCode {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Build descriptive error message\n\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"status code %d (%s) does not match pattern '%s' (expected %d-%d)\",\n\t\tactual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual), pattern, minCode, maxCode)\n}\n\n// parseStatusCodePattern parses a status code pattern like '4xx' and returns the century digit.\n//\n// This helper extracts the century digit from patterns in the format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5.\n// It validates the pattern format and returns an error for invalid patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The status code pattern to parse (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - The century digit (1-5) if the pattern is valid\n//   - error if the pattern is invalid\n//\n// Example:\n//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('4xx')\n//   // century == 4, err == nil\n//\n// Example (invalid pattern):\n//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('invalid')\n//   // century == 0, err != nil\nfunc parseStatusCodePattern(pattern string) (int, error) {\n\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if pattern ends with 'xx'\n\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract the century digit\n\tcentury := int(pattern[0] - '0')\n\n\t// Validate century is in valid HTTP range (1-5)\n\tif century < 1 || century > 5 {\n\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': century digit must be 1-5\", pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn century, nil\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n//\n// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n// - 2xx for success responses (200-299)\n// - 4xx for client errors (400-499)\n// - 5xx for server errors (500-599)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//\n// Example:\n//   // Validate 2xx success response\n//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n//\n//   // Validate 4xx client error\n//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499)\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(t *testing.T, response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif minCode > maxCode {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ValidateStatusCodeRange: minCode (%d) cannot be greater than maxCode (%d)\", minCode, maxCode)\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\tif actualCode < minCode || actualCode > maxCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code in range [%d-%d] (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n\t\t\tminCode, maxCode, getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode),\n\t\t\tactualCode, GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode))\n\t}\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n// in tests. Useful when you need to handle different status codes programmatically\n// rather than failing the test immediately.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the response has the expected status code, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n//       // Handle 404 case\n//   } else {\n//       // Handle other cases\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the response status code is in the allowed list, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   allowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n//   if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n//       // Handle success case\n//   } else {\n//       // Handle error case\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCodeRange checks if the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for range-based status code validation.\n// Useful for category-based status code checking (e.g., 2xx, 4xx, 5xx).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the status code is within the range, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//   // Check for any success code (2xx)\n//   if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n//       // Handle success\n//   }\nfunc CheckStatusCodeRange(response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) bool {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\treturn actualCode >= minCode && actualCode <= maxCode\n}\n\n// GetStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n//\n// This helper provides descriptive names for common HTTP status codes, making\n// test error messages more informative and easier to understand.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - code: The HTTP status code\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - A human-readable description string (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n//\n// Example:\n//   desc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n//   // desc = \"Not Found\"\nfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n\t\t100: \"Continue\",\n\t\t101: \"Switching Protocols\",\n\t\t102: \"Processing\",\n\t\t200: \"OK\",\n\t\t201: \"Created\",\n\t\t202: \"Accepted\",\n\t\t203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n\t\t204: \"No Content\",\n\t\t205: \"Reset Content\",\n\t\t206: \"Partial Content\",\n\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\",\n\t\t301: \"Moved Permanently\",\n\t\t302: \"Found\",\n\t\t303: \"See Other\",\n\t\t304: \"Not Modified\",\n\t\t305: \"Use Proxy\",\n\t\t307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n\t\t400: \"Bad Request\",\n\t\t401: \"Unauthorized\",\n\t\t402: \"Payment Required\",\n\t\t403: \"Forbidden\",\n\t\t404: \"Not Found\",\n\t\t405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\",\n\t\t407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\",\n\t\t409: \"Conflict\",\n\t\t410: \"Gone\",\n\t\t411: \"Length Required\",\n\t\t412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\",\n\t\t414: \"URI Too Long\",\n\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n\t\t416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\",\n\t\t418: \"I'm a teapot\",\n\t\t422: \"Unprocessable Entity\",\n\t\t423: \"Locked\",\n\t\t424: \"Failed Dependency\",\n\t\t426: \"Upgrade Required\",\n\t\t428: \"Precondition Required\",\n\t\t429: \"Too Many Requests\",\n\t\t431: \"Request Header Fields Too Large\",\n\t\t451: \"Unavailable For Legal Reasons\",\n\t\t500: \"Internal Server Error\",\n\t\t501: \"Not Implemented\",\n\t\t502: \"Bad Gateway\",\n\t\t503: \"Service Unavailable\",\n\t\t504: \"Gateway Timeout\",\n\t\t505: \"HTTP Version Not Supported\",\n\t\t506: \"Variant Also Negotiates\",\n\t\t507: \"Insufficient Storage\",\n\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\",\n\t\t510: \"Not Extended\",\n\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n\t}\n\n\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\treturn \"Unknown\"\n}\n\n// getRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n//\n// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges,\n// such as \"Success\" for 2xx codes or \"Client Error\" for 4xx codes.\nfunc getRangeDescription(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n\tranges := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n\t}\n\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Codes %d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n}\n\n// getStatusCode extracts the HTTP status code from various response types.\n//\n// This helper supports both *httptest.ResponseRecorder and *http.Response,\n// making the validation functions work with different response types.\nfunc getStatusCode(response interface{}) int {\n\tswitch r := response.(type) {\n\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n\t\treturn r.Code\n\tcase *http.Response:\n\t\treturn r.StatusCode\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"Unsupported response type: %T. Expected *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response\", response))\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON STATUS CODE CATEGORIES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateSuccessStatusCode validates that the response has a 2xx success status code.\n//\n// This convenience function validates any successful response (200-299).\n// Common success codes include:\n// - (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n//   - httpContext: HTTP context (method, endpoint) for error messages\n//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information including ValidationError\n//\n// Example:\n//   ctx := server.HTTPContext{Method: \"GET\", Endpoint: \"/api/users/123\"}\n//   result := AssertStatusCodeWithHTTPContext(w, 200, ctx, true)\n//   if !result.Match {\n//       t.Error(result.ValidationError.Error())\n//   }\nfunc AssertStatusCodeWithHTTPContext(response interface{}, expectedCode int, httpContext HTTPContext, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\tmatches := actualCode == expectedCode\n\n\t// Format HTTP context for error message\n\tcontextStr := FormatHTTPContext(httpContext)\n\n\tif matches {\n\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\t\tExpectedDescription: validationError.Error(),\n\t\tValidationError:    validationError,\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertStatusCodeAnyWithHTTPContext validates status code against multiple allowed codes with HTTP context.\n//\n// This AssertStatusCodeAnyWithHTTPContext(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int, httpContext HTTPContext, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\",\n\t\t\tResponseContext:     FormatHTTPContext(httpContext),\n\t\t\tError:              \"AssertStatusCodeAnyWithHTTPContext: allowedCodes cannot be empty\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode :=
