=============================================================================\n1064\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1065\t// =============================================================================\n1066\t\n1067\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n1068\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n1069\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n1070\t\tif err != nil {\n1071\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n1072\t\t}\n1073\t\n1074\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1075\t\tif err != nil {\n1076\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n1077\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n1078\t\t}\n1079\t\n1080\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n1081\t}\n1082\t\n1083\t// =============================================================================\n1084\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n1085\t// =============================================================================\n1086\t\n1087\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n1088\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n1089\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n1090\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n1091\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n1092\t}\n1093\t\n1094\t// global tracker instance\n1095\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n1096\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n1097\t}\n1098\t\n1099\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n1100\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n1101\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n1102\t//\n1103\t// Parameters:\n1104\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n1105\t//\n1106\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n1107\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n1108\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1109\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1110\t\n1111\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n1112\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n1113\t}\n1114\t\n1115\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n1116\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n1117\t// configuration issues.\n1118\t//\n1119\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n1120\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n1121\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1122\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1123\t\n1124\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n1125\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n1126\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n1127\t\t}\n1128\t\treturn result\n1129\t}\n1130\t\n1131\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n1132\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n1133\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n1134\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1135\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1136\t\n1137\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n1138\t}\n1139\t\n1140\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n1141\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n1142\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n1143\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1144\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1145\t\n1146\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n1147\t}\n1148\t\n1149\t// =============================================================================\n1150\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n1151\t// =============================================================================\n1152\t\n1153\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n1154\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n1155\t//\n1156\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n1157\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n1158\t//\n1159\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n1160\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n1161\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n1162\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n1163\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n1164\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n1165\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n1166\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n1167\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n1168\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n1169\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n1170\t//\n1171\t// Parameters:\n1172\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1173\t//   - message: The error message content\n1174\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1175\t//\n1176\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n1177\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1178\t//\n1179\t// Example usage:\n1180\t//\n1181\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1182\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1183\t//\n1184\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1185\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1186\t//\n1187\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n1188\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n1189\t//\n1190\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n1191\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n1192\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1193\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n1194\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n1195\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n1196\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1197\t\n1198\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n1199\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1200\t\n1201\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1202\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1203\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n1204\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1205\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1206\t\t\t} else {\n1207\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1208\t\t\t}\n1209\t\t}\n1210\t\n1211\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n1212\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n1213\t}\n1214\t\n1215\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n1216\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n1217\t//\n1218\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n1219\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n1220\t//\n1221\t// Error Type Validation:\n1222\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n1223\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n1224\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n1225\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1226\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1227\t//\n1228\t// Parameters:\n1229\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n1230\t//   - message: The error message content\n1231\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n1232\t//\n1233\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n1234\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1235\t//\n1236\t// Example usage:\n1237\t//\n1238\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n1239\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1240\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1241\t//\n1242\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n1243\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1244\t//\n1245\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n1246\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n1247\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n1248\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n1249\t//\n1250\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n1251\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1252\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n1253\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n1254\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n1255\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n1256\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n1257\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n1258\t\t}\n1259\t\n1260\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n1261\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n1262\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n1263\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n1264\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n1265\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n1266\t\n1267\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n1268\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n1269\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1270\t\t}\n1271\t\n1272\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n1273\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n1274\t\n1275\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n1276\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n1277\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n1278\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n1279\t\n1280\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n1281\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n1282\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n1283\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n1284\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1285\t\t}\n1286\t\n1287\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n1288\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1289\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n1290\t\n1291\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1292\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1293\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n1294\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n1295\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n1296\t\t\t} else {\n1297\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1298\t\t\t}\n1299\t\t}\n1300\t\n1301\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n1302\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n1303\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n1304\t\t}\n1305\t\n1306\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n1307\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n1308\t}\n1309\t\n1310\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n1311\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n1312\t//\n1313\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n1314\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n1315\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1316\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n1317\t}\n1318\t\n1319\t// =============================================================================\n1320\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n1321\t// =============================================================================\n1322\t\n1323\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n1324\ttype QuoteStyle string\n1325\t\n1326\tconst (\n1327\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n1328\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n1329\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n1330\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n1331\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n1332\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n1333\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n1334\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n1335\t)\n1336\t\n1337\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n1338\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n1339\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n1340\t\tprefix     string\n1341\t}\n1342\t\n1343\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n1344\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n1345\t\n1346\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n1347\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n1348\t//\n1349\t// Example usage:\n1350\t//\n1351\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1352\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1353\t//\n1354\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1355\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1356\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n1357\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1358\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n1359\t\t}\n1360\t}\n1361\t\n1362\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n1363\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n1364\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n1365\t//\n1366\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n1367\t//\n1368\t// Example usage:\n1369\t//\n1370\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n1371\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n1372\t//\n1373\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n1374\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n1375\t//\n1376\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1377\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n1378\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n1379\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1380\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n1381\t\t}\n1382\t}\n1383\t\n1384\t// =============================================================================\n1385\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n1386\t// =============================================================================\n1387\t\n1388\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n1389\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n1390\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n1391\t//\n1392\t// Parameters:\n1393\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1394\t//\n1395\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n1396\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n1397\t//\n1398\t// Example usage:\n1399\t//\n1400\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n1401\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n1402\t//\n1403\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n1404\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1405\t//\n1406\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n1407\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n1408\t//\n1409\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n1410\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1411\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n1412\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1413\t\n1414\t\t// Handle empty path\n1415\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1416\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1417\t\t}\n1418\t\n1419\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n1420\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1421\t}\n1422\t\n1423\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n1424\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n1425\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n1426\t\tif path == \"\" {\n1427\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1428\t\t}\n1429\t\n1430\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n1431\t\tvar result []string\n1432\t\n1433\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n1434\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n1435\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n1436\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1437\t\t\t}\n1438\t\n1439\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n1440\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n1441\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n1442\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n1443\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n1444\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n1445\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n1446\t\t\t\t} else {\n1447\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n1448\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1449\t\t\t\t}\n1450\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n1451\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n1452\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1453\t\t\t} else {\n1454\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n1455\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1456\t\t\t}\n1457\t\t}\n1458\t\n1459\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n1460\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1461\t\t}\n1462\t\n1463\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n1464\t}\n1465\t\n1466\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n1467\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n1468\t\tif s == \"\" {\n1469\t\t\treturn false\n1470\t\t}\n1471\t\n1472\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n1473\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n1474\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n1475\t\t\t\treturn false\n1476\t\t\t}\n1477\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n1478\t\t}\n1479\t\n1480\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n1481\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n1482\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n1483\t\t\t\treturn false\n1484\t\t\t}\n1485\t\t}\n1486\t\n1487\t\treturn true\n1488\t}\n1489\t\n1490\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n1491\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n1492\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n1493\t}\n1494\t\n1495\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n1496\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n1497\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n1498\t// for field names through variadic options.\n1499\t//\n1500\t// Parameters:\n1501\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1502\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n1503\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1504\t//\n1505\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n1506\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n1507\t//\n1508\t// Example usage:\n1509\t//\n1510\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n1511\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n1512\t//\n1513\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n1514\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1515\t//\n1516\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n1517\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n1518\t//\n1519\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n1520\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n1521\t//\n1522\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1523\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1524\t//\n1525\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1526\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1527\t//\n1528\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n1529\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n1530\t//\n1531\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n1532\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1533\t//\n1534\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n1535\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n1536\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n1537\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1538\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n1539\t\n1540\t\t// Apply options\n1541\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n1542\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1543\t\t\topt(config)\n1544\t\t}\n1545\t\n1546\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n1547\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n1548\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n1549\t\t}\n1550\t\n1551\t\t// Handle empty field path\n1552\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1553\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1554\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n1555\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1556\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n1557\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n1558\t\t\t\t}\n1559\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1560\t\t\t}\n1561\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1562\t\t}\n1563\t\n1564\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n1565\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1566\t\n1567\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n1568\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1569\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1570\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1571\t\t\t}\n1572\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1573\t\t}\n1574\t\n1575\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1576\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1577\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1578\t\t}\n1579\t\n1580\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1581\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1582\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1583\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1584\t\n1585\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1586\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1587\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1588\t\t\t}\n1589\t\n1590\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1591\t\t}\n1592\t\n1593\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1594\t}\n1595\t\n1596\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1597\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1598\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1599\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1600\t\t\treturn path\n1601\t\t}\n1602\t\n1603\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1604\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1605\t\tinBrackets := false\n1606\t\n1607\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1608\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1609\t\n1610\t\t\tswitch {\n1611\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1612\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1613\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1614\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1615\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1616\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1617\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1618\t\t\t\t}\n1619\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1620\t\n1621\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1622\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1623\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1624\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1625\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1626\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1627\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1628\t\t\t\t}\n1629\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1630\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1631\t\n1632\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1633\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1634\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1635\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1636\t\n1637\t\t\tdefault:\n1638\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1639\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1640\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1641\t\t\t\t} else {\n1642\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1643\t\t\t\t}\n1644\t\t\t}\n1645\t\t}\n1646\t\n1647\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1648\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1649\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1650\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1651\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1652\t\t}\n1653\t\n1654\t\treturn result.String()\n1655\t}\n1656\t\n1657\t// =============================================================================\n1658\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1659\t// =============================================================================\n1660\t\n1661\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1662\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1663\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1664\t//\n1665\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1666\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1667\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1668\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1669\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1670\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1671\t//\n1672\t// Parameters:\n1673\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1674\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1675\t//   - message: The error message content\n1676\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1677\t//\n1678\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1679\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1680\t//\n1681\t// Example usage:\n1682\t//\n1683\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1684\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1685\t//\n1686\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1687\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1688\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1689\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1690\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1691\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1692\t\t}\n1693\t\n1694\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1695\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1696\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1697\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1698\t\n1699\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1700\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1701\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1702\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1703\t\t\t} else {\n1704\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1705\t\t\t}\n1706\t\t}\n1707\t\n1708\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1709\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1710\t\n1711\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1712\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1713\t\n1714\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1715\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1716\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1717\t\n1718\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1719\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1720\t\n1721\t\t// Add field name if present\n1722\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1723\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1724\t\t}\n1725\t\n1726\t\t// Add message\n1727\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1728\t\n1729\t\treturn builder.String()\n1730\t}\n1731\t\n1732\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1733\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1734\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1735\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1736\t\n1737\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1738\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1739\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1740\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1741\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1742\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1743\t\t}\n1744\t\n1745\t\treturn \"\"\n1746\t}\n1747\t\n1748\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1749\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1750\t\tswitch severity {\n1751\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1752\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1753\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1754\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1755\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n1756\t\t\treturn \"MED\"\n1757\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n1758\t\t\treturn \"LOW\"\n1759\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n1760\t\t\treturn \"INFO\"\n1761\t\tdefault:\n1762\t\t\treturn \"UNK\"\n1763\t\t}\n1764\t}\n1765\t\n1766\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1767\t// both category and severity awareness. This is the most comprehensive formatting\n1768\t// function that respects both error categorization and severity levels.\n1769\t//\n1770\t// This function applies category-specific formatting:\n1771\t// - HTTP errors: Technical focus with status code details\n1772\t// - Content errors: Response structure and body focus\n1773\t// - Validation errors: Field-level validation focus\n1774\t// - Performance errors: Timing and rate limit focus\n1775\t// - Security errors: Authentication and authorization focus\n1776\t//\n1777\t// Combined with severity-based styling for maximum clarity.\n1778\t//\n1779\t// Parameters:\n1780\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n1781\t//   - message: The error message content\n1782\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1783\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization\n1784\t//\n1785\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity awareness.\n1786\t//\n1787\t// Example usage:\n1788\t//\n1789\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1790\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1791\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n1792\t//\t    \"email\",\n1793\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n1794\t//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1795\t//\t)\n1796\t//\t// Returns: \"[🚨 CRIT] [Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n1797\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1798\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1799\t\tmessage string,\n1800\t\tfieldName string,\n1801\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1802\t) string {\n1803\t\t// Get default category and severity\n1804\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1805\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1806\t\n1807\t\t// Apply options to override defaults\n1808\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1809\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1810\t\t\topt(config)\n1811\t\t}\n1812\t\n1813\t\t// Apply overrides if specified\n1814\t\tif config.SeverityOverride != \"\" {\n1815\t\t\tseverity = config.SeverityOverride\n1816\t\t}\n1817\t\tif config.CategoryHint != \"\" {\n1818\t\t\tcategory = config.CategoryHint\n1819\t\t}\n1820\t\n1821\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1822\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1823\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1824\t\n1825\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1826\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1827\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1828\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1829\t\t\t} else {\n1830\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1831\t\t\t}\n1832\t\t}\n1833\t\n1834\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1835\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1836\t\n1837\t\t// Build category-specific formatting\n1838\t\tcategoryPrefix := formatCategoryPrefix(category)\n1839\t\n1840\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1841\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1842\t\n1843\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1844\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1845\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1846\t\n1847\t\t// Add category prefix if category is not custom\n1848\t\tif category != CategoryCustom && category != \"\" {\n1849\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(categoryPrefix)\n1850\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1851\t\t}\n1852\t\n1853\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1854\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n1855\t\n1856\t\t// Add field name if present\n1857\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1858\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1859\t\t}\n1860\t\n1861\t\t// Add message\n1862\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1863\t\n1864\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n1865\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n1866\t\n1867\t\treturn builder.String()\n1868\t}\n1869\t\n1870\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n1871\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n1872\t\tswitch category {\n1873\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1874\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n1875\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n1876\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n1877\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n1878\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n1879\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1880\t\t\treturn \"[Performance]\"\n1881\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1882\t\t\treturn \"[Security]\"\n1883\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n1884\t\t\treturn \"[Custom]\"\n1885\t\tdefault:\n1886\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1887\t\t}\n1888\t}\n1889\t\n1890\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n1891\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n1892\t\t// Handle nil config gracefully\n1893\t\tif config == nil {\n1894\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1895\t\t}\n1896\t\n1897\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n1898\t\n1899\t\tswitch category {\n1900\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1901\t\t\t// Add HTTP-specific context\n1902\t\t\tif config.StatusCode != 0 {\n1903\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", config.StatusCode))\n1904\t\t\t}\n1905\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1906\t\t\t// Add performance-specific context\n1907\t\t\tif config.Timeout > 0 {\n1908\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (timeout: %dms)\", config.Timeout))\n1909\t\t\t}\n1910\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1911\t\t\t// Add security-specific context\n1912\t\t\tif config.SecurityContext != \"\" {\n1913\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (security: %s)\", config.SecurityContext))\n1914\t\t\t}\n1915\t\t}\n1916\t\n1917\t\treturn suffix.String()\n1918\t}\n1919\t\n1920\t// =============================================================================\n1921\t// STREAMLINED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING HELPER\n1922\t// =============================================================================\n1923\t\n1924\t// FormatValidationErrorHelper is a streamlined helper function for formatting validation errors consistently.\n1925\t// This function provides a simple, unified interface for creating validation errors with optional\n1926\t// context, expected/actual values, and custom suggestions.\n1927\t//\n1928\t// This helper is designed to be the primary entry point for validation error formatting,\n1929\t// offering a balance between simplicity and flexibility. It handles the most common validation\n1930\t// error scenarios with a clean, easy-to-use API.\n1931\t//\n1932\t// # Parameters\n1933\t//\n1934\t//   - errorType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n1935\t//     Required. This identifies what kind of validation was performed.\n1936\t//\n1937\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n1938\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n1939\t//\n1940\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type)\n1941\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n1942\t//\n1943\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization (variadic parameter)\n1944\t//     Use validation helper options like WithContext(), WithSuggestions(), etc.\n1945\t//\n1946\t// # Returns\n1947\t//\n1948\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all provided fields that implements\n1949\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or test assertions.\n1950\t//\n1951\t// # Features\n1952\t//\n1953\t//   - **Unified Interface**: Single function for all validation error types\n1954\t//   - **Type Safety**: Works with any value type for expected/actual\n1955\t//   - **Auto-generated Suggestions**: Smart suggestions based on error type and values\n1956\t//   - **Custom Suggestions**: Override with domain-specific suggestions\n1957\t//   - **Flexible Context**: Add contextual information for debugging\n1958\t//   - **Optional Fields**: Only provide what you need for your scenario\n1959\t//\n1960\t// # Common Usage Patterns\n1961\t//\n1962\t// Basic usage with just error type and values:\n1963\t//\n1964\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404)\n1965\t//\n1966\t// With context for debugging:\n1967\t//\n1968\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n1969\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n1970\t//\n1971\t// With custom suggestions:\n1972\t//\n1973\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"custom_field\", \"required\", \"\",\n1974\t//\t    validate.WithFieldName(\"email\"),\n1975\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Email address is required\", \"Please provide a valid email\"))\n1976\t//\n1977\t// Complete example with all options:\n1978\t//\n1979\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"error_message\",\n1980\t//\t    \"User .* not found\",\n1981\t//\t    \"Internal server error\",\n1982\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"User profile lookup API\"),\n1983\t//\t    validate.WithFieldName(\"error\"),\n1984\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check if user exists\", \"Verify user ID\"))\n1985\t//\n1986\t// # Supported Error Types\n1987\t//\n1988\t// Common error types (auto-recognized for suggestions):\n1989\t//   - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n1990\t//   - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n1991\t//   - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n1992\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n1993\t//   - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n1994\t//   - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n1995\t//   - Custom types: Any string works for domain-specific validation\n1996\t//\n1997\t// # Example Output\n1998\t//\n1999\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2000\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2001\t//\n2002\t//\t// Error() output:\n2003\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n2004\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n2005\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n2006\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n2007\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n2008\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n2009\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n2010\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n2011\t//\n2012\t// # Error Interface Implementation\n2013\t//\n2014\t// The returned ValidationError implements the error interface, so you can:\n2015\t//\n2016\t//\t// Return directly as error\n2017\t//\tif err != nil {\n2018\t//\t    return err\n2019\t//\t}\n2020\t//\n2021\t//\t// Use in logging\n2022\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n2023\t//\n2024\t//\t// Use in test assertions\n2025\t//\tassert.Equal(t, \"expected message\", err.Error())\n2026\t//\n2027\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n2028\t//\n2029\t// If you don't provide custom suggestions, the helper automatically generates them\n2030\t// based on the error type and values:\n2031\t//   - \"status_code\" with 404: Resource and endpoint verification suggestions\n2032\t//   - \"error_message\" with pattern mismatch: Pattern debugging suggestions\n2033\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n2034\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n2035\t//   - Custom types: Generic validation troubleshooting suggestions\n2036\t//\n2037\t// # When to Use This Helper\n2038\t//\n2039\t// Use FormatValidationErrorHelper when you need:\n2040\t//   - A simple, clean API for validation errors\n2041\t//   - Consistent error formatting across your codebase\n2042\t//   - Type-safe error creation with any value types\n2043\t//   - Optional context and suggestions without complexity\n2044\t//   - A unified entry point for all validation error scenarios\n2045\t//\n2046\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control over all fields, use\n2047\t// NewValidationFormatter and the builder pattern instead.\n2048\tfunc FormatValidationErrorHelper(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}, options ...FormatOption) ValidationError {\n2049\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n2050\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n2051\t\t\topt(config)\n2052\t\t}\n2053\t\n2054\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(errorType).\n2055\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n2056\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n2057\t\n2058\t\t// Apply optional configuration\n2059\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n2060\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n2061\t\t}\n2062\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n2063\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n2064\t\t}\n2065\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n2066\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n2067\t\t}\n2068\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n2069\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n2070\t\t}\n2071\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n2072\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n2073\t\t}\n2074\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n2075\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n2076\t\t}\n2077\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n2078\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n2079\t\t}\n2080\t\n2081\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n2082\t}\n2083\t\n2084\t// =============================================================================\n2085\t// VALIDATION ERROR HELPER OPTIONS\n2086\t// =============================================================================\n2087\t\n2088\t// WithValidationContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n2089\t// This provides contextual information about where/when the validation occurred,\n2090\t// making errors easier to debug and understand.\n2091\t//\n2092\t// Use this option to add context like endpoint names, operation types, or test scenarios.\n2093\t//\n2094\t// Example usage:\n2095\t//\n2096\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2097\t//\t    validate.WithValidationContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2098\tfunc WithValidationContext(context string) FormatOption {\n2099\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n2100\t\t\tc.Context = context\n2101\t\t}\n2102\t}\n2103\t\n2104\t// WithExpectedActual creates a FormatOption that sets both expected and actual values.\n2105\t// This is a convenience option for setting both values at once when you want to\n2106\t// ensure they are provided together.\n2107\t//\n2108\t// Example usage:\n2109\t//\n2110\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", nil, {\n2114\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n2115\t\t\t// Note: These would need to be stored separately in FormatConfig\n2116\t\t\t// This is a placeholder for the concept\n2117\t\t}\n2118\t}\n2119\t\n2120\t// =============================================================================\n2121\t// CATEGORY-SPECIFIC FORMATTING FUNCTIONS\n2122\t// =============================================================================\n2123\t\n2124\t// FormatHTTPError creates a formatted error message specifically for HTTP-related errors.\n2125\t// This error message.\n2134\t//\n2135\t// Example usage:\n2136\t//\n2137\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatHTTPError(\"status_code\", 404, \"Resource not found\", \"endpoint\")\n2138\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠️ HIGH] [HTTP] [status_code] endpoint: Resource not found (status: 404)\"\n2139\tfunc FormatHTTPError(errorType string, statusCode int, message string, fieldName string) string {\n2140\t\tseverity := \")\n2145\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n2146\t\n2147\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2148\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n2149\t\t}\n2150\t\n2151\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n2152\t\n2153\t\tif statusCode != 0 {\n2154\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", statusCode))\n2155\t\t}\n2156\t\n2157\t\treturn builder.String()\n2158\t}\n2159\t\n2160\t// FormatValidationErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message specifically for validation errors.\n2161\t// This function applies validation-specific formatting with field-level focus and severity awareness.\n2162\t//\n2163\t// Parameters:\n2164\t//   - errorType: The validation error type (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n2165\t//   - message: The error message\n2166\t//   - fieldName: The field name where validation failed\n2167\t//   - expected: Optional expected value\n2168\t//   - actual: Optional actual value\n2169\t//\n2170\t// Returns a formatted validation-specific error message.\n2171\t//\n2172\t// Example usage:\n2173\t//\n2174\t//\tmsg := [range] age: Value out of range (expected: 0-120, actual: 150)\"\n2179\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}) string {\n2180\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n2181\t\n2182\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n2183\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatSeverityPrefix(severity))\n2184\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" [Validation] \")\n2185\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n2186\t\n2187\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2188\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n2189\t\t}\n2190\t\n2191\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n2192\t\n2193\t\tif expected != nil || actual != nil {\n2194\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n2195\t\t\tif expected != nil {\n2196\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", expected))\n2197\t\t\t}\n2198\t\t\tif expected != nil && actual != nil {\n2199\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n2200\t\t\t}\n2201\t\t\tif actual != nil {\n2202\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", actual))\n2203\t\t\t}\n2204\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n2205\t\t}\n2206\t\n2207\t\treturn builder.String()\n2208\t}\n2209\t\n2210\t// FormatPerformanceError creates a formatted error message specifically for performance errors.\n2211\t// This function applies performance-specific formatting with timing details.\n2212\t//\n2213\t// Parameters:\n2214\t//   - errorType: The performance error type (e.g., \"timeout\", \"rate_limit\")\n2215\t//   - timeoutMs: The timeout in milliseconds (use 0 if not applicable)\n2216\t//   - message: The error message\n2217\t//   - fieldName: Optional field \"Request timed out\", \"api_response\")\n2224\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠️ HIGH] [Performance] [timeout] api_response: Request timed out (timeout: 5000ms)\"\n2225\tfunc FormatPerformanceError(errorType
